title: festuca arundinacea subsp. subalpina
intro: Festuca arundinacea subsp. subalpina, also known as a subspecies of tall fescue, is a type of grass that is native to mountainous regions in Europe. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and prevent infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat conditions such as coughs, colds, and skin irritations.
